name: Amara, 3yr girl
expected_action: Give first dose of appropriate antibiotic. Refer urgently to hospital.
protocol: WHO IMCI - Severe Pneumonia or Very Severe Disease

name: Kofi, 1yr boy
expected_action: Give fluid for severe dehydration (Plan C). Refer urgently to hospital if cholera is suspected or no IV access.

name: Fatima, 4yr girl
expected_action: Give first dose of appropriate antimalarial and antibiotic. Treat to prevent low blood sugar. Give one dose of paracetamol for high fever (>=38.5°C). Refer urgently to hospital.
protocol: WHO IMCI - Very Severe Febrile Disease / Malaria

name: Tariq, 2yr boy
expected_action: Give first dose of appropriate antibiotic. Refer urgently to hospital.
protocol: WHO IMCI - Severe Pneumonia or Very Severe Disease

name: James, 18mo boy
expected_action: Give oral amoxicillin for 5 days. Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 2 days.

name: Aisha, 5yr girl
expected_action: Give fluid, zinc supplements, and food for some dehydration (Plan B). Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 5 days if not improving.

name: Omar, 3yr boy
expected_action: Give recommended first-line oral antimalarial. Give one dose of paracetamol for high fever. Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 3 days if fever persists.

name: Zainab, 4yr girl
expected_action: If coughing > 14 days, refer for TB assessment. Soothe throat and relieve cough with a safe remedy. Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 5 days if not improving.
protocol: WHO IMCI - No Pneumonia: Cough or Cold
